summary: A single-story rectangular pavilion-like building with a low stepped wooden hipped roof, stone lower walls with a continuous band of windows, and a raised white border/platform surrounding the structure. The roof uses stacked wooden slabs/blocks stepping to a small flat top. Visible decorative stone details include cobblestone corners and stonebrick lower course; windows are 1-block high glass panes in a continuous strip.

shape.overall_form: rectangular block with a stepped low-profile roof and a raised border platform
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along the long axis (mirror left-right); likely symmetric front-back as well

elements: continuous 1-block-high horizontal window strip on long sides
stepped wooden roof with small flat top
wooden floor/internal deck visible from top
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular footprint ~11x21 blocks (estimate from visible proportions); build a 1-block-high stonebrick lower band about 3 blocks tall overall for walls.
Place a continuous row of glass panes centered in the stoneband on the long sides (several 1x windows separated by stonebrick blocks).
Use cobblestone blocks at the four vertical corners replacing the stonebrick on corners for accent.
Add a wooden planked roof built by stacking wooden slab/wood stairs in stepped layers up to a small flat wooden top (hip/stepped shape).
Surround the structure with a 1-block-high white trim/border (quartz blocks) offset one block out from the walls to form a raised perimeter platform.
uncertainties: Exact footprint dimensions (width/depth) are estimated from images; could be off by a few blocks.
Material identifications for white border (assumed quartz) and stone slab roof accents are not 100% certain from textures.
The internal floor layout and any interior walls are not visible; internal features are unknown.
The roof uses wooden slabs/stairs but exact block types (planks vs slabs vs stairs) are approximated as 'wood'.
